title: hypericum montanum var. scaberulum
intro: Hypericum montanum var. scaberulum, also known as Rough St John's Wort, is a variety of flowering plant in the St John's Wort family, native to mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The medicinal properties of Rough St John's Wort have been associated with several health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and immune system support. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
